title: Parameter without default value (--lint-only)
body:
When running Verilator with the `--lint-only` flag, I am getting the following error if I have a parameter without a default value:
> Parameter without initial value is never given value (IEEE 1800-2017 6.20.1)

See the following example from the `test_regress/t` directory:
https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/master/test_regress/t/t_param_noval_bad.v
```
module t #(parameter P);
   generate
      var j;
      for (j=0; P; j++)
        initial begin end
   endgenerate
endmodule
```

In this case, the parameter `P` not having a default value returns the error in question, when just linting the file.

The IEEE Std 1800-2017 LRM in section 6.20.1 states the following:
> In the declaration of a parameter in a parameter port list, the specification for its default value may be
omitted, in which case the parameter shall have no default value.

I understand that it is required for and instantiated module to have a parameter value specified, but I don't think it should be a requirement for every parameter to have a default value.

Only tried with the following version of Verilator, as I think this is more of a question of rules, not a code bug.
`Verilator 5.014 2023-08-06 rev v5.014-35-ge6b0bdd4d`

What OS and distribution are you using?
RHEL 8.8

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?
Sure, but I have never looked at the Verilator code base before. 

Is the solution to disable this check in the `--lint-only` mode?
